variants.description: Get a list of AWS Account Integration Configs.

queryParams.name: aws_account_id
queryParams.description:
Optional query parameter to filter accounts by AWS Account ID.
If not provided, all accounts are returned.

schema.name: data
schema.description: List of AWS Account Integration Configs.

children.name: attributes
children.description: AWS Account response attributes.

children.name: account_tags
children.description: Tags to apply to all hosts and metrics reporting for this account. Defaults to `[]`.

children.name: auth_config
children.description: AWS Authentication config.

fields.name: external_id
fields.description: AWS IAM External ID for associated role.

children.name: aws_account_id
children.description: AWS Account ID.

children.name: aws_partition
children.description:
AWS partition your AWS account is scoped to. Defaults to `aws`.
See [Partitions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/aws-fault-isolation-boundaries/partitions.html)
in the AWS documentation for more information.

children.name: aws_regions
children.description: AWS Regions to collect data from. Defaults to `include_all`.

children.name: created_at
children.description: Timestamp of when the account integration was created.

children.name: logs_config
children.description: AWS Logs Collection config.

children.name: lambda_forwarder
children.description:
Log Autosubscription configuration for Datadog Forwarder Lambda functions.
Automatically set up triggers for existing and new logs for some services,
ensuring no logs from new resources are missed and saving time spent on manual configuration.

children.name: lambdas
children.description: List of Datadog Lambda Log Forwarder ARNs in your AWS account. Defaults to `[]`.

children.name: log_source_config
children.description: Log source configuration.

children.name: tag_filters
children.description: List of AWS log source tag filters. Defaults to `[]`.

children.name: source
children.description: The AWS log source to which the tag filters defined in `tags` are applied.

children.name: tags
children.description: The AWS resource tags to filter on for the log source specified by `source`.

children.name: sources
children.description:
List of service IDs set to enable automatic log collection.
Discover the list of available services with the
[Get list of AWS log ready
services](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/aws-logs-integration/#get-list-of-aws-log-ready-services)
endpoint.

children.name: metrics_config
children.description: AWS Metrics Collection config.

children.name: automute_enabled
children.description: Enable EC2 automute for AWS metrics. Defaults to `true`.

children.name: collect_cloudwatch_alarms
children.description: Enable CloudWatch alarms collection. Defaults to `false`.

children.name: collect_custom_metrics
children.description: Enable custom metrics collection. Defaults to `false`.

children.name: enabled
children.description: Enable AWS metrics collection. Defaults to `true`.

children.name: namespace_filters
children.description: AWS Metrics namespace filters. Defaults to `exclude_only`.

fields.name: exclude_only
fields.description:
Exclude only these namespaces from metrics collection.
Defaults to `["AWS/SQS", "AWS/ElasticMapReduce", "AWS/Usage"]`.
`AWS/SQS`, `AWS/ElasticMapReduce`, and `AWS/Usage` are excluded by default
to reduce your AWS CloudWatch costs from `GetMetricData` API calls.

children.name: tag_filters
children.description: AWS Metrics collection tag filters list. Defaults to `[]`.

children.name: namespace
children.description: The AWS service for which the tag filters defined in `tags` will be applied.

children.name: tags
children.description: The AWS resource tags to filter on for the service specified by `namespace`.

children.name: modified_at
children.description: Timestamp of when the account integration was updated.

children.name: resources_config
children.description: AWS Resources Collection config.

children.name: cloud_security_posture_management_collection
children.description:
Enable Cloud Security Management to scan AWS resources for vulnerabilities, misconfigurations,
identity risks, and compliance violations. Defaults to `false`.
Requires `extended_collection` to be set to `true`.

children.name: extended_collection
children.description:
Whether Datadog collects additional attributes and configuration information about the resources
in your AWS account. Defaults to `true`. Required for `cloud_security_posture_management_collection`.

children.name: traces_config
children.description: AWS Traces Collection config.

children.name: xray_services
children.description: AWS X-Ray services to collect traces from. Defaults to `include_only`.

children.name: id
children.description:
Unique Datadog ID of the AWS Account Integration Config.
To get the config ID for an account, use the
[List all AWS integrations](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/aws-integration/#list-all-aws-integrations)
endpoint and query by AWS Account ID.

children.name: type
children.description: AWS Account resource type.

examples.name: Example

entries.description: List all AWS integrations curl example
entries.code:
# Set your Datadog site and credentials
export DD_SITE="datadoghq.com"
export DD_API_KEY="<DD_API_KEY>"
export DD_APP_KEY="<DD_APP_KEY>"

curl -X GET "https://api.datadoghq.com/api/v2/integration/aws/accounts?aws_account_id=123456789012" \
-H "DD-API-KEY: ${DD_API_KEY}" \
-H "DD-APPLICATION-KEY: ${DD_APP_KEY}" \
-H "Accept: application/json"

entries.code:
"""
List all AWS integrations returns "AWS Accounts List object" response
"""

from datadog_api_client import ApiClient, Configuration
from datadog_api_client.v2.api.aws_integration_api import AWSIntegrationApi

configuration = Configuration()
with ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
    api_instance = AWSIntegrationApi(api_client)
    response = api_instance.list_aws_accounts()

    print(response)

# List all AWS integrations returns "AWS Accounts List object" response

require "datadog_api_client"
api_instance = DatadogAPIClient::V2::AWSIntegrationAPI.new
p api_instance.list_aws_accounts()

variants.description: **This endpoint is deprecated - use the V2 endpoints instead.** List all Datadog-AWS integrations available in your Datadog organization.

queryParams.name: account_id
queryParams.description: Only return AWS accounts that matches this `account_id`.

queryParams.name: role_name
queryParams.description: Only return AWS accounts that matches this role_name.

queryParams.name: access_key_id
queryParams.description: Only return AWS accounts that matches this `access_key_id`.

children.name: access_key_id
children.description: Your AWS access key ID. Only required if your AWS account is a GovCloud or China account.

children.name: account_id
children.description: Your AWS Account ID without dashes.

children.name: account_specific_namespace_rules
children.description:
An object (in the form `{"namespace1":true/false, "namespace2":true/false}`) containing user-supplied overrides
for AWS namespace metric collection. **Important**: This field only contains namespaces explicitly configured through API calls,
not the comprehensive enabled or disabled status of all namespaces. If a namespace is absent from this field, it uses Datadog's
internal defaults (all namespaces enabled by default, except `AWS/SQS`, `AWS/ElasticMapReduce`, and `AWS/Usage`).
For a complete view of all namespace statuses, use the V2 AWS Integration API instead.

children.name: cspm_resource_collection_enabled
children.description: Whether Datadog collects cloud security posture management resources from your AWS account. This includes additional resources not covered under the general `resource_collection`.

children.name: excluded_regions
children.description:
An array of [AWS regions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints)
to exclude from metrics collection.

children.name: extended_resource_collection_enabled
children.description: Whether Datadog collects additional attributes and configuration information about the resources in your AWS account. Required for `cspm_resource_collection`.

children.name: filter_tags
children.description:
The array of EC2 tags (in the form `key:value`) defines a filter that Datadog uses when collecting metrics from EC2.
Wildcards, such as `?` (for single characters) and `*` (for multiple characters) can also be used.
Only hosts that match one of the defined tags
will be imported into Datadog. The rest will be ignored.
Host matching a given tag can also be excluded by adding `!` before the tag.
For example, `env:production,instance-type:c1.*,!region:us-east-1`

children.name: host_tags
children.description:
Array of tags (in the form `key:value`) to add to all hosts
and metrics reporting through this integration.

children.name: metrics_collection_enabled
children.description: Whether Datadog collects metrics for this AWS account.

children.name: resource_collection_enabled
children.description: Deprecated in favor of 'extended_resource_collection_enabled'. Whether Datadog collects a standard set of resources from your AWS account.

children.name: role_name
children.description: Your Datadog role delegation name.

children.name: secret_access_key
children.description: Your AWS secret access key. Only required if your AWS account is a GovCloud or China account.

-H "DD-API-KEY: ${DD_API_KEY}" \

-H "Accept: application/json"

entries.code:
"""
List all AWS integrations returns "OK" response
"""

from datadog_api_client import ApiClient, Configuration
from datadog_api_client.v1.api.aws_integration_api import AWSIntegrationApi

configuration = Configuration()
with ApiClient(configuration) as api_client:
    api_instance = AWSIntegrationApi(api_client)
    response = api_instance.list_aws_accounts()

    print(response)

# List all AWS integrations returns "OK" response

require "datadog_api_client"
api_instance = DatadogAPIClient::V1::AWSIntegrationAPI.new
p api_instance.list_aws_accounts()
